one_line_thesis: Horizon Kinetics–affiliated holding company with concentrated stakes in exchange infrastructure (MIH) and HKHC — a look-through bet on Murray Stahl's ecosystem, not an operating croupier; value = sum-of-parts vs fair-value accounting noise.

deep_dive.executive_summary: FRMO is a permanent-capital vehicle tied to the Horizon Kinetics ecosystem. Book value per share is about $8.55; the OTC quote near $6.70 is roughly 22% below book with negligible debt and high insider ownership. Reported earnings are noisy because unrealized investment gains drove Q3 FY2026 net income; that is not operating cash flow. A ten-year free-cash-flow model is the wrong primary tool here. Our Lawrence base case uses a five-year sum-of-parts catalyst path: payoff $18 per share from $6.70 implies about 21.9% per year, with total synthesis near 26% per year after qualitative adjustme...

pricing_analysis.conclusion: Hurdle prices are derived from the decision-grade universal contract; they do not constitute a capital decision.

decision.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

change_control.rule: A source fact is locked. Every assumption change requires a reason, author, timestamp, and before/after value.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_fit.label: Scarce assets, royalties, and optionality
applicability_tests: The security owns a legally enforceable economic claim on scarce assets or royalties.
Material value is omitted or poorly represented by current GAAP earnings.
Asset quantities, ownership, overlap, timing, and remaining capital can be reconciled.
Comparable values can be adjusted for location, quality, control, liquidity, and realization time.
failure_modes: gross asset value mistaken for shareholder value
operating cash flow and NAV counted twice
remote options treated as contracted cash

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: The current component schedule is the first decision-grade baseline; attribution begins with the next dated valuation snapshot.

component_valuation.decision_rule: Use the complete low/base/high component schedule. Embedded components are estimated but not added twice.

timeline.label: Q3 FY2026 book $8.55/sh; Investment A 82% of equity; MIH fair value $13.9M post-IPO lockup
Synteq Digital stake received ($1.843M) from North Carolina asset sale and related-party transfer
MIAX (MIH) IPO completed; FRMO holds fair-value stake with six-month lockup
CMSG begins OTCQX trading; FRMO elects fair-value accounting
FY2025 book $7.26/sh; HKHC financials anchor royalty and equity-method marks
FRMO renamed from FRM Nexus (1993 incorporation); ongoing Horizon affiliate structure
Winland (WELX) accumulation path begins per approved Substack; control >50% remains open
SSI frictionless flywheel: step-wise HK ecosystem stakes (MIAX, HKHC, CMSG, Winland)
Permanent-capital holdco model: no fund redemptions; concentrated Horizon book

industry.structure: FRMO is a non-operating holdco; economics flow from Horizon Kinetics advisory royalties and mark-to-market stakes in exchanges, funds, and land/crypto affiliates. No product revenue; OTC liquidity is thin.
industry.share_shift: Value accrues to listed affiliates (MIAX IPO 2025, HKHC public) and pending listings (CMSG OTCQX); Investment A dominates book at ~82% of FRMO equity.
industry.trend: Mark volatility from equity securities ($574M fair value) drives reported earnings; catalyst path is affiliate re-rates and Winland operating consolidation, not shell operating margin.

top_claims.claim: FRMO is a permanent-capital vehicle tied to Murray Stahl's Horizon ecosystem. Book value per share is about $8.55; the OTC quote near $6.70 (Stooq, May 22, 2026) is roughly 22% below book with negligible debt and high insider ownership. Reported earnings are noisy because unrealized investment gains drove Q3 FY2026 net

*Primary risk: Investment A concentration (~82%) and fair-value mark-downs if MIH lockup, fund losses, or risk-off markets hit the consolidated position. [Fact] On January 15, 2026 FRMO announced that prior annual and certain interim financial statements should no longer be relied upon pending a deferred tax restatemen
Horizon franchise impairment: royalty stream shrinks.
